scenario: FR-017 lineage + concurrency: (dedup) <=1 in-flight review per lineage; (out-of-order) an obsolete completion whose digest != current is superseded; (final-corr 1) same-digest authority is an atomically-acquired LINEAGE LEASE / generation — ONLY the lease owner is authoritative, a conflicting non-owner/stale-generation result FAILS CLOSED, and a clean result never erases blocking findings by timestamp; (final-corr 3) the lineage id CANONICALIZES its inputs (resolved anchor commit id + canonical target) so aliases resolving to the same commit are equivalent; (corr 1) the auto-fire baseline is the last-ACCEPTED reviewed TREE.
note: Lineage is keyed by the STABLE resolved anchor commit + target. The lease replaces the earlier max-run-id tie-break so a later clean run cannot erase an earlier blocking run by timestamp. Contract predicates under test are PURE.

lineage_id_canonicalization.note: Get-ContinuousCoReviewLineageId takes the RESOLVED anchor commit id (caller does git rev-parse; never HEAD/branch) + canonical target. Aliases that resolve to the SAME commit id (any case/whitespace) yield the SAME id; a different target yields a different id.

auto_fire_baseline.note: the next auto-fire diffs advance from the LAST ACCEPTED reviewed TREE (a tree-id); git ancestry uses commit refs separately.

cases.name: dedup-second-fire-does-not-launch
cases.why: run1 is already in flight for this lineage; wait/poll it, never a duplicate.

cases.name: different-lineage-fire-may-launch
cases.why: no in-flight review exists for a DIFFERENT lineage.

cases.name: out-of-order-older-completion-superseded
cases.why: an older-digest completion never matches the current tree; superseded.

cases.name: current-digest-completion-not-stale
cases.why: digest matches the current tree (a necessary condition; authority is then decided by the LEASE, below).

same_digest_lease.note: TWO terminal runs complete for the SAME current digest (a genuine race). The LEASE was acquired before launch; only its owner is authoritative. A non-owner or stale-generation completion FAILS CLOSED. A clean result never erases the blocking findings already recorded.

authority_cases.name: owner-same-generation-authoritative
authority_cases.why: the completing run IS the lease owner for the current generation.

authority_cases.name: non-owner-fails-closed
authority_cases.why: a DIFFERENT run at the same digest is not the lease owner; it never becomes authoritative by timestamp — fail closed, reconcile explicitly.

authority_cases.name: stale-generation-fails-closed
authority_cases.why: even the owner run fails closed if it carries a stale generation (a superseded lease).

blocking_preservation_cases.name: clean-does-not-erase-existing-blocking
blocking_preservation_cases.why: a clean incoming result must NEVER erase the 2 existing blocking findings by timestamp; they persist until explicitly resolved.

blocking_preservation_cases.name: no-existing-blocking-nothing-to-preserve
blocking_preservation_cases.why: no blocking findings existed; nothing is erased either.
